NOTICE OF REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE (TRANSACTION ID # 62903153) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GOLDMAN, DAVID ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED WILD RIVERS TRANSPORT LLC ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED LOPEZ, JOSEPH

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID GOLDMAN, WILD RIVERS TRANSPORT LLC, AND JOSEPH LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO SHOW EITHER A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OR AN IMMINENT THREAT OF IRREPARABLE HARM. (SAN FRANCISCO NEWSPAPER PRINTING CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 170 CAL.APP.3D 438, 442 [HOLDING THAT AN INJUNCTION MUST NOT ISSUE UNLESS IT IS REASONABLY PROBABLE THAT THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS]; TAHOE KEYS PROPERTY OWNERS' ASSN. V. STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BD. (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 1459, 1471 [HOLDING THAT ISSUING A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST A PUBLIC AGENCY REQUIRES A "SIGNIFICANT SHOWING OF IRREPARABLE INJURY"].) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT). ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew Kumin, Esq. 415-655-7494 for Plaintiff.
Jorge Aguilar, Deputy Attorney General, 510-879-0002 of the Office of the Attorney General for Defendant Californa Highway Patrol.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court share it's views. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Defendant argues.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAVID GOLDMAN, WILD RIVERS TRANSPORT LLC, JOSEPH LOPEZ's Motion For A Preliminary Injunction.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs David Goldman, Wild Rivers Transport LLC, and Joseph Lopez's motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiffs have failed to show either a reasonable probability of success on the merits or an imminent threat of irreparable harm. (San Francisco Newspaper Printing Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 438, 442 [holding that an injunction must not issue unless it is reasonably probable that the moving party will prevail on the merits]; Tahoe Keys Property Owners' Assn. v. State Water Resources Control Bd. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1471 [holding that issuing a preliminary injunction against a public agency requires a "significant showing of irreparable injury"].) (see word minutes for ful details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
